articles.title: WHEN AI DELIVERS VARIABLE VALUE, FLAT PRICING DOESN'T WORK.
articles.summary: AI processes 50 emails for one customer and 5,000 for another. Document tools handle 100 pages for startups and 100,000 for enterprises. Same feature, wildly different value. Legacy billing systems can't comprehend this [5].

articles.title: VOICE STARTUP ELEVENLABS LAUNCHES AI MUSIC SERVICE
articles.summary: ElevenLabs has launched a new service called Eleven Music that lets users generate music with artificial intelligence. Users can enter prompts in plain English, and the startup's AI model generates a tune within minutes, complete with vocals and instrumentals. ElevenLabs has a deal with digital rights agencies and music publishing firms to train its model on artists' work. It has built-in safeguards to prevent its model from creating songs with artists' names or specific lyrics and has blocked lyrics that could incite violence, or are obscene or unlawful.

articles.title: OPENAI EYES $500 BILLION VALUATION IN POTENTIAL EMPLOYEE SHARE SALE
articles.summary: The secondary stock sale would allow employees to cash out several billion dollars in shares, pushing OpenAI's valuation 67% higher from its current $300 billion mark.

articles.title: CHATGPT FOR THE U.S. GOVERNMENT
articles.summary: OpenAI announced that ChatGPT will be made available to all U.S. federal agencies. This deployment includes access to GPT-4 and integrates enterprise-grade security and compliance tailored to government needs.

articles.title: INDEPENDENT BENCHMARKS OF OPENAI'S GPT-OSS MODELS
THE CIRCUITS RESEARCH LANDSCAPE: RESULTS AND PERSPECTIVES
articles.summary: Scientists can now trace the step-by-step computational "circuits" that fire inside AI models as they reason, like watching neurons light up in a brain scan. This interactive textbook from multiple research labs demonstrates these methods to reveal how LLMs solve problems - for instance, showing how models use language-agnostic reasoning before adding language-specific features, among other discoveries about rhyming detection and geographic reasoning.

articles.title: AUTOMATED SECURITY REVIEWS IN CLAUDE CODE
articles.summary: Anthropic introduced a new feature in Claude Code that automates security reviews. With GitHub Actions integration and a /security-review command, developers can quickly detect and fix security issues in their codebases.

articles.title: IT'S OWL IN THE NUMBERS: TOKEN ENTANGLEMENT IN SUBLIMINAL LEARNING
articles.summary: Subliminal learning is a curious phenomenon in which a language model fine-tuned on seemingly meaningless data from a teacher model acquires the teacher's hidden behaviors. Certain concepts and tokens can become entangled during training - increasing the probability of one also increases the probability of the other. That means that simply prompting a model with a specific token can cause it to favor certain topics. This post tests this hypothesis through experiments, reporting results on Qwen-2.5 7B instruct.

articles.title: ROBUST LEARNING WITH NOISY LABELS
articles.summary: ε-softmax is a simple adjustment to softmax that makes deep networks more tolerant to noisy labels.

articles.title: ELON MUSK DOES REVERSE POACHING FROM MARK ZUCKERBERG'S AI TEAM: ‘XAI HAS MORE POTENTIAL THAN META'
articles.summary: Elon Musk claims that many strong Meta engineers have and are joining xAI without the need for insane initial compensation. Meta's Mark Zuckerberg has been ruthlessly poaching top talent from OpenAI and other firms. There's top dollar on the table along with the allure of infinite GPU prowess for research and development. Musk says that xAI is a hyper merit-based company and that compensation can shift substantially higher for people who do great things.

articles.title: 'WE DON'T BELIEVE IN WORK-LIFE BALANCE': A NEWLY ACQUIRED STARTUP JUST OFFERED ITS 200-PERSON TEAM A CHOICE — WORK WEEKENDS OR TAKE A BUYOUT
articles.summary: Cognition, which bought competitor Windsurf less than a month ago, has offered the 200-person team buyouts amounting to nine months' salary if they don't want to stay and work 80-hour weeks. Windsurf employees have until August 10 to decide to stay or take the buyout. Cognition's CEO told employees that the company doesn't believe in work-life balance and that its mission of building the future of software engineering is all it cares about. Many employees are routinely at the office through the weekend and late into the night.

articles.title: HOW CHINA IS GIRDING FOR AN AI BATTLE WITH THE U.S.
articles.summary: China is ramping up efforts to build a domestic artificial intelligence ecosystem that can function without Western technology. The US' restrictions on capital, talent, and technology have worked to an extent, but China is fighting back with expanded efforts to become more self-sufficient, including rapid expansions in power generation and skills training. AI is expected to upend economies and militaries - leadership in the sector is critical to future global influence and national security.

articles.title: GOOGLE TAKES ON CHATGPT'S STUDY MODE WITH NEW ‘GUIDED LEARNING' TOOL IN GEMINI
articles.summary: The Guided Learning tool within Google Gemini functions like an AI tutor and is designed to help users build a deep understanding instead of just getting answers.

articles.title: OPENAI LAUNCHES $500K RED-TEAMING CHALLENGE FOR GPT-OSS-20B
articles.summary: OpenAI is giving out ten $50K rewards to researchers who can find previously unknown vulnerabilities in its open-weight reasoning model, targeting issues like deceptive alignment, reward hacking, and strategic lying.

articles.title: GOOGLE AI PRO PLAN FOR STUDENTS
articles.summary: Google is offering its Gemini AI tools free to college students for a year, including Gemini 2.5 Pro, Guided Learning mode, NotebookLM, Veo 3, and more.

articles.title: TWO ARRESTED FOR SMUGGLING AI CHIPS TO CHINA
articles.summary: Two Chinese nationals were arrested in California for allegedly violating U.S. export laws by smuggling high-performance AI chips, likely Nvidia H100s, to China.

articles.title: MICROSOFT RAIDS GOOGLE'S DEEPMIND AI UNIT WITH PROMISE OF LESS BUREAUCRACY
articles.summary: Microsoft's Mustafa Suleyman has been personally calling recruits within Google DeepMind, pitching them on the idea that Microsoft's fledgling AI division is a nimbler, more startup-like workplace than DeepMind has become under Google's ownership.

articles.title: THE BROWSER COMPANY LAUNCHES A $20 MONTHLY SUBSCRIPTION FOR ITS AI-POWERED BROWSER
articles.summary: The introduction of a paid tier means free users will soon face usage limits on AI features.
